evals.prompt: Run a sector brief for the Retail & Apparel sector
evals.expected_output: Single fenced ```html code block containing a complete standalone HTML document built from the skill's assets/template.html scaffold. All 12 core sa-* IDs populated (cover title, date, footer date, overview title, exec, overview, macro, financial, structure, risks, outlook, sources). Section 1 has >= 4 subsection-title headers, Section 2 >= 3, Section 3 >= 3 (with concrete $/€/£/% numeric metrics), Section 4 >= 4 (with Porter's Five Forces rated High/Moderate/Low for each force), Section 5 >= 5. Section 6 states Positive/Stable/Negative in paragraph-only form. Executive Summary is paragraph-only. >= 8 inline cite-ref citations, >= 6 source-item entries.
assertions: Output is a complete standalone HTML document (doctype + </html>)
HTML uses the skill's template scaffold (cite-ref / subsection-title / source-item CSS classes)
Section 3 contains >= 5 concrete numeric metrics ($/€/£/% values)
Section 4 includes Porter's Five Forces with High/Moderate/Low ratings (>= 5 rating tokens)
Section 6 (Outlook) is paragraph-only and states Positive/Stable/Negative
Inline citations use cite-ref class on <a> or <span> tags (>= 8 occurrences), preferring clickable <a class="cite-ref" href="..."> when the source has a URL
Sources list contains >= 6 entries (source-item divs or <li>)

evals.prompt: I need a deep-dive on the Aerospace & Defense sector — give me the full sector overview, financials, risks, and outlook
evals.expected_output: Same structural requirements as eval-1, applied to Aerospace & Defense. Tests natural-language phrasing and a non-consumer sector where procurement budgets, backlog, and DoD appropriations substitute for retail spending metrics.
assertions: Output is a complete standalone HTML document (doctype + </html>)
HTML uses the skill's template scaffold (cite-ref / subsection-title / source-item CSS classes)
Section 3 contains >= 5 concrete numeric metrics ($/€/£/% values)
Section 4 includes Porter's Five Forces with High/Moderate/Low ratings (>= 5 rating tokens)
Section 6 (Outlook) is paragraph-only and states Positive/Stable/Negative
Inline citations use cite-ref class on <a> or <span> tags (>= 8 occurrences), preferring clickable <a class="cite-ref" href="..."> when the source has a URL
Sources list contains >= 6 entries (source-item divs or <li>)

evals.prompt: Generate a sector brief report for Banking
evals.expected_output: Same structural requirements as eval-1, applied to a financial-services sector where bank-specific metrics (NIM, ROAA, CET1, deposits) substitute for generic corporate margin/leverage metrics. Tests sector flex (Banking vs Retail/A&D).
assertions: Output is a complete standalone HTML document (doctype + </html>)
HTML uses the skill's template scaffold (cite-ref / subsection-title / source-item CSS classes)
Section 3 contains >= 5 concrete numeric metrics ($/€/£/% values)
Section 4 includes Porter's Five Forces with High/Moderate/Low ratings (>= 5 rating tokens)
